contact.title: Tell me what you're building.
contact.description: If you'd like to reach out, this form is the most direct way. Tell me who you are, what you're working on, or what you need help solving.
contact.direct: You can also email me at nicoabgv@gmail.com or take a look at my code on GitHub.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Hi Nicolás, I'm reaching out because...

meta.title: Nicolás Álvarez · Software Engineer
meta.description: Minimal portfolio by Nicolás Álvarez. Software engineer specialized in SaaS platforms, automation, and security-focused products.
meta.ogDescription: Explore business platforms, SaaS products, and music tools built with modern stacks and robust processes.
meta.twitterDescription: Software engineer. I build scalable, automated, production-ready products.

hero.taglineHtml: Software engineer · Product · Automation
hero.title: I'm Nicolás Álvarez, software engineer.
hero.description: I design and build digital products with a focus on clarity, automation, and technical execution. Most of my work sits in SaaS, internal tools, and software that needs to perform well in production.
hero.summaryTitle: I turn ideas into web products that are solid, clear, and ready to use.

summaryItems.first: Python backend with Flask and FastAPI for systems that need order, automation, and reliability.
summaryItems.second: Frontend with React and Tailwind when the product needs a clear, fast, and maintainable experience.
summaryItems.third: Product-minded technical judgment to turn ideas into real solutions.

about.label: Profile
about.title: I build software with a product mindset and a strong technical foundation.
about.paragraph1: I work across backend, frontend, and automation, with a strong focus on products that need structure, clarity, and sound technical decisions from the start. Most of my work lives in SaaS, internal tools, and operational flows where improvisation quickly becomes expensive.
about.paragraph2: Beyond shipping code, I like shaping ideas: understanding the problem, deciding where complexity is worth it, and turning that into maintainable software. Security is part of that way of working, not as decoration, but as practical judgment.

skills.label: What I do
skills.title: How I usually add value inside a project.

backend.title: Backend and automation
backend.description: I design APIs, internal processes, and automations so the product can handle real usage without relying on manual patches.

frontend.title: Interface and experience
frontend.description: I build clean, understandable interfaces, making sure the product feels fast, clear, and easy to evolve.

devops.title: Practical infrastructure
devops.description: I set up simple, reliable environments with Docker, Nginx, and automation that help teams deploy and maintain without friction.

security.title: Applied security
security.description: I bring authentication, data, and deployment best practices into the build process as a natural part of development, not an afterthought.

projects.label: Featured projects
projects.title: Solutions that support real-world operations.
projects.ecosystemHtml: MesasYa, MiCartaYa, and OrderYa are part of the YaSolutions ecosystem, a line of digital solutions for hospitality and operations. I present them here from the perspective of product, architecture, and technical execution.

mesasya.title: MesasYa
mesasya.description: A comprehensive booking platform that eliminates operational chaos. Digitizes the floor plan and automates customer contact.

bullets.first: -40% management time via intelligent automatic table assignment.
bullets.second: Drastic reduction in 'no-shows' through an automated reminder system.
bullets.third: Proven scalability: handling peak demand across multiple locations in real time.

mesasya.directAccess: → MesasYa – Managing +50 restaurants and automated reservations.

caseStudy.nextTag: Automatic report integration and management experience optimization.

micartaya.title: MiCartaYa
micartaya.description: Digital ecosystem transforming the ordering experience. Unifies QR menus with automatic kitchen printing.

bullets.first: Instant ordering via WebSockets: from table to kitchen in under a second.
bullets.second: Real-time stock control: immediate synchronization between menu and inventory.
bullets.third: Zero transcription errors: direct integration with POS printing systems.

micartaya.directAccess: → MiCartaYa – Real-time ordering and multi-language digital menu.

caseStudy.nextTag: Integrated payments and customer loyalty features.

orderya.title: OrderYa
orderya.description: Order centralization for bakeries. Replaces spreadsheets with an automated production and delivery flow.

bullets.first: -30% data entry time by eliminating informal order channels (WhatsApp).
bullets.second: Automatic label and delivery sheet generation by route and customer.
bullets.third: 100% precision in daily production based on consolidated actual demand.

orderya.directAccess: → OrderYa – Order centralization and automation for bakeries.

caseStudy.nextTag: Demand prediction based on historical data and holidays.

trato.title: Trato
trato.description: Agile CRM designed to close sales. Prioritizes opportunity tracking via automated reminders.

bullets.first: Accelerated pipeline: clear visualization of each quote's lifecycle.
bullets.second: Smart reminders: Celery handles follow-ups so no deal is lost.
bullets.third: Secure access control through a robust invitation code system.

trato.directAccess: → Trato – Agile CRM with smart reminders and optimized pipeline.

caseStudy.nextTag: WhatsApp API integration for direct follow-ups.

kobra.title: Kobra
kobra.description: Financial management platform for Cybersur. Automates billing cycles and payment alerts.

bullets.first: Improved cash flow through automated client alert workflows.
bullets.second: Full audit: total traceability of payments and financial statuses.
bullets.third: +20% operational efficiency for the finance team by eliminating repetitive tasks.

beatlist.title: BeatList
beatlist.description: Advanced music analytics suite. Uses the official API to offer metrics that Spotify doesn't show by default.

bullets.first: Smart data cleaning: duplicate removal and audio filter algorithms.
bullets.second: Guaranteed security: official authentication via OAuth 2.0 validated by Spotify.
bullets.third: High-impact UX: interactive dashboards with real-time audio analytics.

beatlist.directAccess: → BeatList – Officially approved advanced Spotify analytics.

caseStudy.nextTag: Recommendations based on related artist graphs.

ponme.title: Ponme
ponme.description: Wardrobe digitization through multimodal IA. Analyzes garments and generates combinations based on context.

bullets.first: AI-driven outfits based on specific plans and weather variables.
bullets.second: Device-side image processing to guarantee privacy and speed.
bullets.third: Usage tracking system to help rotate clothes and improve sustainability.

ponme.directAccess: → Ponme – Your intelligent fashion assistant powered by Gemini AI.

caseStudy.nextTag: 'Virtual Fitting Room' mode with augmented reality (AR).

kobra.title: Financial platform supporting Cybersur daily operations.
kobra.paragraph1: Kobra is the custom solution I built for Cybersur. With a FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker stack, it centralizes billing, payments, and notifications so the finance team works with coherent, up-to-date information.
kobra.paragraph2: Scheduled cronjobs manage data exports and cleaning, while alert and reminder pipelines log every action. The entire flow is designed for immediate visibility and to reduce manual, repetitive tasks.

bullets.first: Automatic workflows that drastically reduce follow-up and collection times.
bullets.second: Traceable cronjobs for exports and cleaning routines to keep data consistent.
bullets.third: Internal dashboards with essential metrics for tracking payments and pendings.

kobra.next: Next iteration: expand internal financial reports and automate reconciliations.
kobra.diagramContent:
Cybersur Team
    ↓
FastAPI API → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery workers → Emails & Reminders
    └─ Scheduled Cronjobs → CSV Reports

beatlist.title: Professional playlist management with the official Spotify seal.
beatlist.paragraph1: BeatList is the platform for music lovers, curators, and creators who need something more powerful than the native Spotify manager. It brings together advanced administration, discovery, and analytics in a seamless experience.
beatlist.paragraph2: Thanks to official integration with the Spotify API and approved OAuth 2.0 authentication, the Flask backend accesses extended data with professional stability and delegates heavy analysis to Celery to keep the experience smooth. The JavaScript frontend offers dashboards and a web player with custom micro-interactions.

bullets.first: Merge, clean up, and reorganize playlists with smart rules based on metrics like energy or danceability.
bullets.second: Recommendation engine by favorite artists and genres, plus dashboards showing listening habits.
bullets.third: Analyses handled by Celery to process data in the background and offer precise metrics without affecting user experience.

beatlist.next: Possible evolutions: add collaborative mode or insight exports in future versions.
beatlist.diagramContent:
Web Client
    ↓
Flask API (OAuth 2.0 Spotify)
    ↓
Celery workers → Heavy analysis & metrics
    ↓
JS Dashboards + Player

mesasya.title: Smart reservations for restaurant chains.
mesasya.paragraph1: MesasYa is a SaaS platform in production that digitizes reservation management for restaurants with multiple locations. It offers granular occupancy control, communication automation, and manager-ready dashboards.
mesasya.paragraph2: With Flask, Celery, MySQL and Docker as the base, the solution is deployed with Docker Compose and security best practices, maintaining customizable workflows by zone, service, and team. Analytics and exports adapt to business decisions.

bullets.first: Interactive floor plans configurable by location, zone, and service.
bullets.second: Automatic notifications for internal teams and end customers.
bullets.third: Demand, capacity, and performance indicators exportable for strategic decisions.

mesasya.next: Next steps: integrate automatic reports and continue improving the manager experience.
mesasya.diagramContent:
Staff and online bookings
    ↓
Flask API → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery workers → Emails & SMS
    └─ Docker Compose → Coordinated services

orderya.title: Unified production and delivery management.
orderya.paragraph1: Built with FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker for maximum speed, it allows managing complex catalogs, prices, and users with differentiated roles. All deployed securely and scalably.
orderya.paragraph2: The platform centralizes orders, production, and delivery with a technical base designed for day-to-day speed, clarity, and scalability.

bullets.first: Unified order portal that eliminates transcription errors.
bullets.second: Automatic generation of production sheets and delivery notes.
bullets.third: Robust infrastructure with Docker ready for multiple clients.

orderya.next: Evolution: Future integration with billing systems.

trato.title: Agile CRM for quotes, follow-ups, and reminders.
trato.paragraph1: Trato is the lightweight CRM that centralizes quotes, follow-ups, and reminders in a fast flow. On a FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker stack deployed with Uvicorn, it lets sales teams work without friction.
trato.paragraph2: Invite codes control company and user sign-ups, while Celery tasks automate reminders and daily summaries. The panel prioritizes operational speed on desktop and mobile.

bullets.first: Guided quote entry with customizable statuses and opportunity comments.
bullets.second: Automatic reminders managed by Celery and internal notifications.
bullets.third: Containerized deployment on the main VPS with backups and basic monitoring.

trato.next: Next steps: expand conversion metrics and integrate automatic reports for management.
trato.diagramContent:
Invited Companies
    ↓
FastAPI + Uvicorn → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery workers → Reminders
    └─ Docker Compose → Main VPS

micartaya.title: 360º management of the restaurant experience.
micartaya.paragraph1: MiCartaYa is a comprehensive solution to digitize interaction between the restaurant and the customer. Based on FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker and two-way communication, it makes order flow instant and error-free.
micartaya.paragraph2: The system stands out for its remote printing engine that connects the cloud with physical kitchen printers, and an administration panel that allows managing complex menus with videos and multi-language support dynamically.

bullets.first: Real-time order reception with latency under 1 second thanks to WebSockets.
bullets.second: Total stock control and product availability with immediate reflection in the public menu.
bullets.third: Centralized management of multiple sedes, allergens, and visual brand customization.

micartaya.next: Next steps: payment gateway integration and support for home delivery.
micartaya.diagramContent:
Diner (QR) / Staff
    ↓
FastAPI (WebSockets) → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Printer Client (Local) → Kitchen Ticket
    └─ Dashboard (React) → Notifications

ponme.title: AI applied to smart personal style management.
ponme.paragraph1: Ponme democratizes use of generative AI to solve the daily 'what do I wear' dilemma. It integrates the Google Gemini API to understand not just what clothes you have, but how to combine them by context.
ponme.paragraph2: The app processes images locally to remove backgrounds and uses multimodal models to extract garment metadata (color, fabric, style), allowing you to create a 'digital wardrobe' in minutes.

bullets.first: Smart outfit generation based on specific plans and weather variables.
bullets.second: Device-side image processing to guarantee privacy and speed.
bullets.third: Usage tracking system to help rotate garments and improve sustainability.

ponme.next: Next steps: smart packing planner and purchase suggestions based on closet gaps.
ponme.diagramContent:
User (Mobile/PWA)
    ↓
FastAPI → PostgreSQL
    │
    ├─ Google Gemini API → Image Analysis
    └─ Rembg (Local) → Garment Segmentation

meta.title: Case Studies · Nicolás Álvarez
meta.description: Detailed case studies for MesasYa, Trato, Kobra, and BeatList. Dive into the architecture, automation, and results of each product.
meta.ogDescription: Technical and business details for MesasYa, Trato, Kobra, and BeatList.
meta.twitterDescription: Dive into MesasYa, Trato, Kobra, and BeatList with architecture, flows, and roadmap.

hero.label: Case studies
hero.title: From vision to deployment: complete product stories.
hero.description: Every project starts from a different challenge. Here you will find the architecture decisions, operational flows, and learnings that allowed launching reliable and measurable solutions.

links.mesasya: → MesasYa – SaaS reservation management platform for restaurants.
links.micartaya: → MiCartaYa – Digital menu and online ordering.
links.trato: → Trato – Agile CRM for quotes, follow-ups, and reminders.
links.beatlist: → BeatList – Advanced Spotify stats and playlist management.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: If you'd like to share a thought about these projects, feel free to write to me.
contact.description: I share these case studies to show how I think about product and architecture. If something resonates with you, I'd be glad to hear from you.

mesasya.label: Case study 01
mesasya.title: MesasYa — Multi-location SaaS Reservations
mesasya.paragraph1: MesasYa is a SaaS platform in production that digitizes booking management for restaurants with multiple locations. It brings interactive floor plans, customizable rules, and real-time notifications so every service runs without surprises.
mesasya.paragraph2: The Flask, Celery, MySQL and Docker backend uses Docker Compose and security best practices. Each restaurant configures zones, services, languages, and specific workflows without losing stability or traceability.

bullets.first: Floor plans configurable with rules by location, zones, and special events.
bullets.second: Automatic table assignment and real-time notifications for staff and customers.
bullets.third: BI-ready exports and metrics that follow business decisions.

mesasya.nextText: Automatic report integration and management experience optimization.
mesasya.schemaContent:
Web Bookings / staff
    ↓
Flask API → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery workers → Emails/SMS
    └─ Docker Compose coordinates services

micartaya.label: Case study 02
micartaya.title: MiCartaYa — Full Table Digitization
micartaya.paragraph1: MiCartaYa redefines restaurant interaction through a SaaS platform in production for digital menus and self-managed ordering. It solves the manual order-taking bottleneck and ensures menu information is always up to date.
micartaya.paragraph2: The core of the project is a high-availability WebSockets system connecting diners to the restaurant dashboard and printing hardware, eliminating transcription errors and optimizing service times.

bullets.first: Drastic reduction in wait time for orders and payments.
bullets.second: Dynamic management of prices and availability with a single click.
bullets.third: Automatic kitchen and bar ticket printing via TCP/IP and USB protocols.

micartaya.nextText: Integrated payments from table and customer loyalty features.
micartaya.schemaContent:
Diner (Mobile)
    ↓
Nginx → FastAPI (WS)
    │
    ├─ PostgreSQL / Redis
    └─ Printer Script (Local) → POS

orderya.label: Case study 03
orderya.title: OrderYa — Comprehensive Order Management
orderya.paragraph1: OrderYa eliminates order management chaos between bakeries and points of sale. It replaces spreadsheets and scattered messages with a centralized platform where each shop orders intuitively and the bakery sees consolidated demand.
orderya.paragraph2: Developed with FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker and server-side rendering (Jinja2) for speed and simplicity, the system handles complex catalogs, price lists by customer, and automatic delivery order generation.

bullets.first: Shop portal with real-time history and status.
bullets.second: Barcode scanning to speed up order creation.
bullets.third: Template creation, product management, and data export.

status.title: Status
status.description: In production. Handling daily orders for actual bakeries.

orderya.schemaContent:
Shops
    ↓
Nginx → FastAPI
    ↓
MySQL (Persistence)
    ↓
Production (PDF lists/View)

trato.label: Case study 04
trato.title: Trato — Agile CRM for Sales Teams
trato.paragraph1: Trato centralizes quotes, follow-ups, and reminders in a lightweight CRM for sales teams. With a FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker backend running with Uvicorn, it prioritizes response speed and clarity for every opportunity.
trato.paragraph2: Companies access via invite codes and manage pipelines with customizable statuses, interaction timelines, and automatic alerts. Celery tasks coordinate reminders, email sends, and daily summaries from the main VPS.

bullets.first: Controlled company and user sign-ups through invite codes.
bullets.second: Follow-ups with automatic reminders and centralized timeline per opportunity.
bullets.third: Containerized deployment on main VPS with backups and Celery task monitoring.

status.title: Status
status.description: Production on main VPS with basic monitoring and daily backups.

trato.schemaContent:
Invited Companies
    ↓
FastAPI + Uvicorn → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery workers → Reminders and emails
    └─ Docker Compose → Main VPS
trato.directAccess: → Trato – Agile CRM for quotes, follow-ups, and reminders.

kobra.label: Case study 05
kobra.title: Kobra — Financial Platform for Cybersur
kobra.paragraph1: Kobra is the custom solution maintaining Cybersur financial operations. It automates billing, payments, and reminders from a robust FastAPI, Celery, MySQL and Docker backend.
kobra.paragraph2: Scheduled cronjobs manage exports, data cleaning, and automatic reconciliations. Internal panels prioritize collection metrics and SLA by client so the finance team has daily clarity.

bullets.first: Automatic reminders and follow-up reducing delinquency.
bullets.second: Cronjobs and data cleaning pipelines with full audit.
bullets.third: Internal dashboards with collection metrics and client SLAs.

kobra.nextText: Automation of financial reports and reconciliations.
kobra.schemaContent:
Cybersur Ops
    ↓
FastAPI → MySQL
    │
    ├─ Celery → Emails
    └─ Cronjobs → Reports

beatlist.label: Case study 06
beatlist.title: BeatList — Music Suite for Creators
beatlist.paragraph1: BeatList follows curators and creators who need to go beyond the standard Spotify manager. It connects the Spotify API with advanced analysis and interactive experiences approved by Spotify for Developers.
beatlist.paragraph2: The Flask backend with OAuth 2.0 retrieves extended data and coordinates analysis with Celery to generate metrics without blocking users. The frontend offers players, dashboards, and mixing tools for pros and communities.

bullets.first: Smart playlist reorganization with energy and rhythm metrics.
bullets.second: Dynamic dashboards and player with interactive experiences.
bullets.third: Heavy processing with Celery keeping insights updated on listening habits.

beatlist.nextText: Collaborative mode and advanced exports in the roadmap.
beatlist.schemaContent:
Users
    ↓
Flask API (OAuth Spotify)
    ↓
Celery workers → Heavy analysis and insights
    ↓
Dashboards + Player

ponme.label: Case study 07
ponme.title: Ponme — Personal Style & AI Assistant
ponme.paragraph1: Ponme is a B2C platform applying multimodal models for personal wardrobe management. It allows users to digitize their clothes and receive smart outfit suggestions generated by Gemini.
ponme.paragraph2: Using local computer vision techniques for image segmentation and advanced LLM reasoning for style combination, Ponme offers a seamless experience that optimizes use of existing garments.

bullets.first: AI-suggested combinations considering event context and local weather.
bullets.second: Automatic garment cataloging (type, color, material) via visual analysis.
bullets.third: PWA mode for a mobile-native experience with fast load and offline functionality.

ponme.nextText: Circular purchase recommendation algorithms and social fashion sharing.
ponme.schemaContent:
User (PWA)
    ↓
FastAPI → PostgreSQL
    │
    ├─ Rembg API → Background
    └─ Google Gemini → Analysis
